channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: Toei Games announces first three published titles – KILLA, HINO, and DEBUG NEPHEMEE
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Games by talented creators from Japan and abroad.

Following the announcement of its establishment last week, Toei Games has announced the first three games it plans to publish—KILLA from Black Tangerine, HINO from UnGloomStudio, and DEBUG NEPHEMEE from Nephemee Studio.

“‘Story’ to Toei does not mean plotline,” the company said in a press release. “It is the summation of captivating characters and a world view that overwhelms the audience. Anything that has a strong and distinctive presence, falls within our definition of ‘story.’ Please enjoy this lineup of completely new and original works, brought to you by Toei in collaboration with talented creators from Japan and abroad.”

Here is an overview of each game, via Toei Games:

KILLA

  • Platform: PC (Steam)
  • Developer: Black Tangerine
  • Release Date: 2026

KILLA is a 3D adventure mystery with multiple endings. Valhalla, our protagonist, seeks the killer of her mentor, a mentor she treasures like family, venturing to an unknown island. Discover the culprit on this enigmatic island and embark on a journey of revenge.

Set in a nightmarish, dark, and fantastical puppet show world, KILLA is a mystery adventure. The player must use their “resonance” ability to delve into the suspects’ memories and piece together fragments of dreams to weave together the truth.

Who is the “La” who killed her mentor? The curtain rises now on this beautifully cruel stage

HINO

  • Platform: PC (Steam)
  • Developer: UnGloomStudio
  • Release Date: TBD

Join Hino and the whimsical Monimoni Skeleton as they traverse a shadow-cloaked world in this atmospheric 2D dark fantasy adventure.

Illustrated in ballpoint pen by the artist Yatara, the world of Hino comes to life as a game. In a shadow-drenched realm overrun by eerie monsters, the red-ribboned Hino and the quirky white Monimoni Skeleton set out on a journey in search of a safe haven. It is in your hands to guide them to that place of peace.

Through lands crawling with monsters and traps, you will uncover a single truth behind the creation of this dark world. When that truth is revealed, your choices will decide which of the many endings their journey will reach.

DEBUG NEPHEMEE

  • Platform: PC (Steam)
  • Developer: Nephemee Studio
  • Release Date: TBD

DEBUG NEPHEMEE is a top-down 2D adventure game set in a place called “Nepherum.”

Here, the world is infested with bugs who inhabit creatures called Nephemees.

Through a unique battle system based on each Nephemees values and philosophies, you will attack and hack opponents to find traces of their personalities and memories.

Encounter a colorful cast of characters and get to know them deeply, as you embark on a journey to “Debug” the world.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/toei-games-announces-first-three-published-titles-killa-hino-and-debug-nephemee
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: TMNT: Splintered Fate Dev Says "There's More To Come" As Roguelike Action Game Hits Major Sales Milestone
item.content:encoded:
Cowabunga!

The roguelike brawler Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Splintered Fate, which we described as a fun and approachable 'Hades-like' when it arrived on the Switch in 2024, has this week hit a new sales milestone.

In an announcement on social media, the developer Super Evil Megacorp has revealed the game has now surpassed a combined total of one million sales worldwide. This includes not only the Switch version, but also sales of the enhanced Switch 2 version.

At the end of the message, the developer also notes how "there's more to come":

Super Evil Megacorp: "THANK YOU for running, brawlin’, and getting back up for one more run with us. Drop a slice of pizza if you're part of the million. Cowabunga, there’s more to come!"

This title has already received countless updates since it arrived on the Switch, including new character updates, new content and also some paid DLC. It also got a Gold Edition in March of this year.

If you haven't already tried it out on the Switch or the Switch 2, it's currently 90% off its regular price on the eShop in the US and UK. There's also a Switch 2 upgrade for existing owners. And if you want to know more about this game, check out our review:

Have you tried out this game on the Switch or Switch 2 yet? Are there any updates you would like to see? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/tmnt-splintered-fate-dev-says-theres-more-to-come-as-roguelike-action-game-hits-major-sales-milestone
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ubisoft Confirms "Original" Assassin's Creed Black Flag Will Still Be Available
item.content:encoded:
Resynced is a "2026 take on the original legend"

Although Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced is skipping the Switch 2 this July, we do have some good news about the original game that fans are sure to appreciate.

According to Ubisoft Singapore and Black Flag Resynced creative director Paul Fu, the new version of the swashbuckling adventure is a "2026 take on the original legend", meaning the existing versions of Black Flag across various digital platforms won't be going anywhere.

This was revealed alongside the news that Resynced would not include multiplayer or the DLC:

Paul Fu: "With Resynced we made a clear choice, it is a pure story driven adventure and we are fully focused on Edward's adventures in the Carribean. As a result of this focus, we have elected to not have the multiplayer and not have the DLC. However, Resynced is built from the ground up, with new story, new content and news sytems, but we're staying true to his action-adventure roots. Resynced is a 2026 take on the original legend, and for those of us who are curious, the original will still be available."

So, there you go, Black Flag fans - if you still want to pick up the original pirate-themed experience in the future on a select digital store, you shouldn't have to worry about it being delisted.

Switch and Switch 2 owners can currently access the original Assassin's Creed IV Black Flag title via the Rebel Collection, which was released in 2019. Here's what we had to say about it at the time:

"Assassin's Creed IV: Black Flag looks and plays phenomenally well on Switch, and is the complete package with all DLC included...Black Flag is a revelation in portable mode and looks and performs almost flawlessly as you blast your way around the Caribbean on Edward Kenway's captivating pirate adventures."

Have you played the original Black Flag game on the Switch? Glad to hear it will still be available when Resynced arrives this July? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/ubisoft-confirms-original-assassins-creed-black-flag-will-still-be-available
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: SUNSOFT announces HARD EDGE real-time tactical card battler remake Hard Edge: War Zone for PC
item.content:encoded:
SUNSOFT has announced Hard Edge: War Zone, a four-versus-four real-time tactical card battler remake of the 1998-released PlayStation action adventure game Hard Edge (known as T.R.A.G. in North America). It will launch for PC via Steam in 2026 with English, French, Italian, German, Spanish, Japanese, Korean, Traditional Chinese, and Simplified Chinese language options.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page and official website:

About

HARD EDGE is back as a four-versus-four real-time tactical card battler! Combine strategic deck-building with simultaneous team actions. Every turn, draw cards to shoot, throw, or defend. Sacrifice cards to move in a high-stakes tactical “WAR ZONE.”

Read the Field, Shoot Your Cards!

Inheriting the gritty sci-fi world of the 1998 classic HARD EDGE, this title boldly reimagines the gameplay as a real-time tactical card battler. Engage in intense four-versus-four team-based combat with up to eight players!

Game Overview

Set in the hard-boiled cyberpunk world of the original HARD EDGE, this is an all-new tactical experience that blends the tension of a shootout with the strategic depth of a card game. Form a four-man squad and utilize your hand to annihilate the enemy team. Your tactics and teamwork will be put to the test in a war zone where turn-based deliberation meets real-time action.

Game System: Real-Time Tactical Card Battle

Combat progresses in turns. At the start of each turn, draw cards and choose your next move. The defining feature is that “all players on the same team act simultaneously.” Sync your timing with allies to suppress enemies with a barrage of fire, or have one player act as a decoy while the rest launch a surprise flank. Coordinated execution is the key to victory.

The Core of Strategy: Deck Building and Mid-Match Growth

Deck building—the heart of any card game—is vital here. Before the match, construct a unique deck tailored to your character’s personality and your personal playstyle. Furthermore, as characters level up during the match, you can add new cards to your deck or enhance existing ones. Victory requires both careful preparation and the ability to adapt your deck to the ever-changing battlefield.

Dominate the War Zone With Diverse Cards

Your deck contains various weapons, equipment, and support items. Choose the best card for the situation:

  • Ranged (Assault Rifles / Handguns / Sniper Rifles) – Powerful ranged attacks. You can snipe enemies in a straight line, but cover like walls will block your shot. Positioning for a clear line of sight is crucial.
  • Throwing (Grenades, etc.) – Deals area-of-effect damage. Perfect for flushing out enemies behind walls or wiping out clustered foes.
  • Combat (Knives, etc.) – High-risk, high-reward melee attacks that deal devastating damage if you can get close enough.
  • Defense (Armor, etc.) – Reduces incoming damage. An essential card for those serving as the team’s shield on the front lines.
  • Support and Tactics -Infinite tactical possibilities with “Medical Kits” to heal allies, “Extended Mags” to boost ammo, and “Deployable Cover” to create safety anywhere.
  • Movement – Positioning is key. Movement does not require a specific card; instead, you can discard ANY card from your hand to move. However, that card is lost. Will you discard your best attack card to escape danger, or stand your ground to save it for later? This “Ultimate Dilemma” defines the strategy of every step.
  • ❮
  • ❯

Two Tactical Game Modes

Adapt your strategy to the objective and lead your team to glory:

  • Capture the Flag – Compete for “Disks” containing vital data. The team with the most Disks when time runs out wins. Efficiency, route planning, and role distribution are more important than simple elimination.
  • Team Deathmatch – A pure, high-octane battle for dominance. The team with the most kills wins. Your combat skills, coordination, and card timing will decide your fate.

Story: The Prequel—“The Sakuraville Occupation”

Six months before the events of the original game… The “Sakuraville” research facility, owned by Machinery Gear, is suddenly seized by an unidentified armed group. Among the hostages is the high-profile scientist, Dr. Kevin. To resolve the crisis and rescue the doctor, authorities covertly deploy an elite special forces unit into the building. Among the members are Alex and Michelle—two individuals destined to be swept up in a harsh fate. Inside the sealed facility, heavy armaments and deadly traps await. Amidst clashing agendas, the desperate rescue operation begins now!

Build your deck, coordinate with allies, and survive the WAR ZONE!

Characters

  • Alex Barrat – AD 2046—an elite special operations agent deployed to the MG Corp. building siege. A consummate professional who prioritizes speed and efficiency above all, operating without emotion. All records are classified—her only calling card is an unidentified, trusted firearm.
  • Michelle Stevenson – An operative in the same unit as Alex. Excels in close-quarters combat with a high-frequency blade, backed by unmatched hand-to-hand skill within the ranks. Her strikes—fluid, yet relentless—shatter enemy defenses, leaving blue wings to cut across the battlefield.
  • Rachel Howard – A girl who holds the key to research buried in the shadows of a powerful corporation—her presence almost otherworldly. Though swept up in brutal conflict, she rises with unwavering resolve, a single light of hope in the WAR ZONE. Her innocent gaze stirs the humanity long forgotten by the operatives on the battlefield.
  • Burns Byford – A hardened man who infiltrated the MG building alone in search of a missing woman. Driven by a detective’s instincts and raw strength, he fights on with stubborn resolve to bring back what he’s lost. Behind his easygoing demeanor lies top-tier skill—he’ll break the chain of corruption with his own fists.
  • Kevin Howard – A former researcher in the bio-weapon development division, now imprisoned within MG Corp’s classified sector. Haunted by the realization that the intelligence he created has become a spark capable of destabilizing the world. Consumed by guilt and despair, he endures in isolation—driven to put an end to the tragedy he set in motion.
  • Cassandra Dupont – One of the masterminds behind the siege. She lived undercover as an office worker at MG Corp—her true nature, a cold beauty driven by a lust for control. Wielding brainwashing techniques and shapeshifting at will, she manipulates humans as mere “parts” to serve her desires.
  • Gasshu Heiworse – The mercenary leader behind the building’s takeover. His body fully converted into a cyborg, he crushes intruders with overwhelming firepower and defense. Unleashing a storm of destruction—ked by his built-in weapon, the “Gash Beam”—this berserker reduces all opposition to ash.
  • Brennum Arkley – One of the mercenaries occupying the building. Equipped with extendable bio-blades in both arms, capable of slicing through metal like paper. Employing tactics such as restraint and transformation, this ruthless hound never lets a captured target escape.

Watch some footage (without sound) from the official website below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Website Background Footage (No Sound)



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/sunsoft-announces-hard-edge-real-time-tactical-card-battler-remake-hard-edge-war-zone-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Asmodee USA Announces Neuroscape Distribution Partnership, Bringing Crowdfunded Card Game to More Players
item.content:encoded:
Asmodee has announced a distribution deal with the developers of the Neuroscape TCG. Thanks to this deal, the cyberpunk sci-fi card game will expand from an initial Kickstarter success to getting a mass release at both retail stores and on Amazon.

The Neuroscape TCG Asmodee USA Distribution Deal

 In an official press release, Asmodee USA announced it would be the primary distribution partner for mass retail and Amazon in the United States for the Neuroscape TCG. In addition, Asmodee USA will be a distribution partner within the hobby channel on a non-exclusive basis, supporting the launch and expansion of the game's product line, including its first expansion: Genesis.

This distribution deal will include the Genesis Unlimited product line. This means the 2-Player Starter Kit featuring the Cybernetic and Hacker factions, booster packs, and collector gift bundles will be widely available for purchase. 

“Neuroscape TCG represents exactly the kind of opportunity we look for when expanding our TCG distribution portfolio, a game built on genuine community passion, exceptional game design, and the kind of competitive depth that keeps players coming back,” said Julien Sharp, Country Manager, asmodee US. “The Kickstarter numbers speak for themselves, but what really caught our attention was the quality of the community Neuroscape has built and the creative integrity behind every card. We are proud to support Neuroscape’s growth in the U.S. market and bring the game to a wider audience of players.”

This partnership is a major milestone for the developers of Neuroscape. The card game made waves when it launched on Kickstarter in May of 2025, raising over $570,000 in the process. The game was warmly received by players, praised for its tactical gameplay, its commitment to showcasing human artwork in a landscape plagued by AI slop, and detailing a rich original sci-fi setting through its art and mechanics.

That support has slowly expanded beyond the campaign's initial backers. The developers have already partnered 200 hobby stores into an organized play program in the first month since its initial release. It's a testament to how the game, while lacking the high profile of other games like Netrunner or the crowdfunding mega success of the Cyberpunk TCG, is built on strong gameplay and good word of mouth.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/asmodee-usa-announces-neuroscape-distribution-partnership-bringing-crowdfunded-card
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hades II Is Getting Another Update Soon, Here Are The Full Patch Notes
item.content:encoded:
Following a Switch 1 & 2 game update last week

Last week, Supergiant Games rolled out a new update for Hades II on the Switch 1 & 2 adding bonus content, fixing multiple issues and making some adjustments.

Now, the development team has announced a new hotfix for the title. According to the team's official social media account, it will be coming in an update "relatively soon" for console players.

"We just released Hotfix 2 with various fixes and other minor changes to last week's Hades II patch! Console players, these improvements will be coming in a patch relatively soon. Thank you for playing and all your support!"

Here's what you can expect when it does arrive on the Switch 1 & 2 (note: patch notes may contain spoilers):

Hades II Post-Launch Patch 2 - Hotfix 2 (Coming soon)

During Dream Dives , increased the chance of Boons being offered in Shops in Tartarus or the Summit if either of these is the first or second region
· During Dream Dives, slightly increased how much Gold you will earn from Encounters in Tartarus or the Summit if either of these is the first or second region
· During Dream Dives, Chronos and Unrivaled Chronos have slightly reduced Life
· During Dream Dives, Charybdis Tentacles in the Unrivaled Scylla Encounter have reduced Life
· Fixed certain sound FX playing louder than expected
· Fixed Braid of Atlas (Charon) not improving Cast damage as expected for most Olympian Cast effects
· Fixed Hazard Boom (Icarus) not dealing damage to Melinoë as expected
· Fixed Proper Upbringing (Hera) continuing to apply its effect if you fell below the element requirement
· Fixed the effect of Anvil Ring (Hephaestus) being interrupted by the effect of Death Defiance
· Fixed sometimes gaining Rallying Cry (Ares) from All Together (Hera) prior to having met Ares
· Fixed Success Rate (Hermes) sometimes being offered if you have Profuse Bleeding (Ares), in light of the rework in Post-Launch Patch 2
· Fixed Moon Beam (Selene) sometimes not raising the likelihood of finding a Path of Stars while using Aspect of Selene if you equipped it between Regions
· Fixed Shimmering Moonshot (Daedalus - Staff) not increasing Omega Special damage as expected
· Fixed foes in Thessaly sometimes starting to attack too soon if you had Corrosion on Sight (Medea)
· Fixed the effect of Vow of Void (Oath) not persisting as expected after a Chaos Trial
· Fixed the effect of Rarify persisting on Boons of Chaos over multiple attempts at the same Chaos Trial
· Fixed an interaction between Rapid Onslaught (Daedalus - Blades) and Latest Model (Icarus)
· Fixed an interaction between Mirrored Thrasher (Daedalus - Staff) and Flood Gain (Poseidon)
· Fixed an interaction between Extra Dose (Apollo) and certain strikes from the Black Coat
· Fixed an interaction between Exceptional Talent (Apollo) and Aspect of Anubis (Staff)
· Fixed an interaction between Cardio Gain (Hestia), Wave Flourish (Poseidon), and the Sister Blades
· Fixed Softness upgrade for Total Eclipse (Selene) unexpectedly activating Origination (Arcana)
· Fixed text for Death Warrant (Artemis) not displaying an improved chance from Success Rate (Hermes)
· Fixed flames from Lubber barrels interrupting the Psychic Leash from Aspect of Circe (Staff)
· Fixed Satyr Supplicants not playing their death animations as expected when slain by Winter Harvest (Demeter) during the Unrivaled Chronos Encounter
· Fixed Melinoë being unable to use Attacks for longer than expected if grabbed by Polyphemus while using the Moonstone Axe Omega Attack
· Fixed the option to Alter offerings in a Pool of Purging becoming inactive if you purchased all goods from a Well of Charon in the same Location
· Fixed Melinoë confiding to Frinos about having had a rough night after a restful Dream Dive
· Fixed a certain instance where Hermes would not talk to Melinoë when expected, preventing entry to the final battle on the surface route that night
· Fixed certain conversations with Moros occurring unexpectedly if Melinoë declines his advances
· Fixed a certain conversation with Hephaestus occurring sooner than expected
· Fixed Headmistress Hecate commenting on the Aspect of Selene when you did not have it equipped
· Fixed a couple of cases in which Dora could not be interacted with when expected
· Fixed letterbox artwork remaining visible at certain resolutions after interacting with the Music Maker
· Fixed an issue where closing the Steam Overlay by clicking the X button locked Melinoë into her Sprint
· Fixed a visual issue related to Melinoë's Sprint animation when striking and sprinting in different directions using mouse-and-keyboard input
· Fixed a visual issue where the marked-target indicator during Heracles encounters could remain visible during Melinoë's Return to Shadow sequence
· Fixed a visual issue when rapidly switching from using Special to Attack using Aspect of Nergal (Axe)
· Fixed a visual issue where foes could rotate unexpectedly after being stunned by Hecuba
· Fixed incorrect portrait appearing in rare instances when speaking to Persephone
· Fixed incorrect portrait appearing when Chronos speaks in Tartarus during Dream Dives
· Fixed some out-of-context voice lines in the Unrivaled Typhon Encounter during Dream Dives
· Fixed a visual issue that could occur during Melinoë's exit from Elysium after vanquishing Zagreus
· Fixed several rare crashes
· Other minor fixes

This update follows a hotfix released last week on PC on 16th April 2026. Here's what it included:

Hades II Post-Launch Patch 2 - Hotfix 1

· Fixed various rare crashes reported since Post-Launch Patch 2
· Fixed an issue where you could not interact with Nemesis while a Night Bloom servant was present
· Fixed one of Aphrodite's new dialogue events occurring earlier than intended
· Fixed rewards appearing out of bounds during Dream Dives in the chamber before the final battle on The Summit if you happened to activate Gold Gold Gold (Echo)
· Fixed a rare progression-blocking issue that could occur if you cleared the Surface route before ever accepting a Boon of Hera
· Other minor fixes

When this next Hades II update is officially released for the Switch 1 & 2 versions of the game, we'll let you know.

[source store.steampowered.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/hades-ii-is-getting-another-update-soon-here-are-the-full-patch-notes
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: A New Version Of Game Pass Coming To Discord Nitro Just Leaked
item.content:encoded:
Microsoft teased a new partnership with Discord earlier this week that led some players to wonder if the popular chat platform’s paid Nitro subscriptions would start getting bundled with Game Pass. It turns out the opposite is happening. According to the latest leaks, a new tier of the Xbox service called Game Pass “Starter Edition” is coming to Discord users in the near future.

Images of the newest version of Game Pass were discovered and shared by Discord Previews (via The Verge) and point to some of the benefits of Xbox’s Netflix-like game library becoming part of Discord’s existing $10 a month Nitro subscription. Game Pass “Starter Edition” will reportedly give Discord users access to over 50 games, including Stardew Valley and Fallout 4, as well as up to 10 hours of cloud game streaming a month.

Discord Nitro ($9.99) Subscribers can soon enjoy free access to Xbox Game Pass (Starter Edition).

This includes:
– Access to over 50 games from the game pass library
– 10 hours a month of cloud game streaming
– Xbox rewards pic.twitter.com/84a0l80mFl

— Discord Previews (@DiscordPreviews) April 23, 2026

xCloud open-source developer Redphx spotted new code suggesting the Starter Edition tier would include access to third-party games in addition to Microsoft’s first-party lineup. The current version of Game Pass Essential, which the Starter Edition seems to be based on, currently offers mostly perennial favorites like Hades and Warhammer: Vermintide 2 rather than new releases.

The leaks come a month after Xbox CEO Asha Sharma met with Netflix co-CEO Greg Peters about ways to bundle Game Pass with other subscriptions, and just a week after Microsoft announced day-and-date Call of Duty releases would be removed from Game Pass Ultimate to bring down its price. The company is reportedly exploring other ways of changing the service to make it more affordable and win over new subscribers.

Sharma released an internal memo on Thursday laying out her vision for Xbox and how it should navigate an increasingly competitive landscape. She pointed to daily active users as the most important metric, something that will certainly go up by bundling Game Pass with Nitro.

It’s unclear if the price of that subscription will go up to compensate or if this is a temporary experiment to see how the math works out for both companies. Microsoft was in talks to buy Discord for $10 billion back in 2021. It ended up buying Activision Blizzard for $70 billion instead.



https://kotaku.com/xbox-game-pass-discord-nitro-stardew-valley-fallout-4-2000690262
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Leak reveals new Xbox Game Pass ‘Starter Edition’ that’s part of Discord Nitro
item.content:encoded:
Tom Warren

is a senior correspondent and author of Notepad, who has been covering all things Microsoft, PC, and tech for over 20 years.

Microsoft started teasing a mysterious Discord and Xbox Game Pass partnership yesterday, and a new leak appears to have detailed exactly what’s coming. Discord Previews has uncovered a new Xbox Game Pass “Starter Edition” that is bundled with Discord Nitro.

The Starter Edition reportedly includes “access to over 50 games from the Game Pass library,” as well as 10 hours a month of Xbox Cloud Gaming streaming and the ability to earn Xbox Rewards points while playing games. It’s not clear which games will be part of the more than 50 titles available, but the leaked images suggest that Stardew Valley, Grounded, and Fallout 4 will be included.

The Starter Edition doesn’t appear to be limited to Microsoft’s own first-party Xbox games, either. Better xCloud developer redphx also uncovered the Xbox Game Pass (Starter Edition) earlier today, and noted that a lot of third-party games will be available through this new Game Pass tier.

I reported in February that Microsoft is looking at ways to bundle third-party services with Game Pass subscriptions. This new Starter Edition appears to be a way for other services to bundle Game Pass. Netflix co-CEO Greg Peters told The Information last month that he and Sharma had “kicked around ideas” for partnering on subscription bundles, so perhaps we’ll see Netflix offer a similar Game Pass bundle soon.

Follow topics and authors from this story to see more like this in your personalized homepage feed and to receive email updates.

  • Tom Warren


https://www.theverge.com/news/917880/xbox-game-pass-starter-edition-discord-nitro-bundle
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1048)—Answers for Friday, April 24
item.content:encoded:
That’s the end of another week! I hope you’ve had a brilliant few days and that your NYT Connections streak is going strong. Today, we can take that one step further and produce yet another win. Below, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to get today’s puzzle ticked off as quickly as possible.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (April 24)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • TOMATO SAUCE
  • HELICOPTER
  • TAXI
  • INTELLIGENCE
  • ICE SKATES
  • RED WINE
  • GRASS
  • LAWN MOWER
  • INK
  • CABIN
  • DOUGH
  • SUCTION CUPS
  • CHEESE
  • CALLOWAY
  • ARMS
  • PEPPERONI

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Things you’d associate with pizza
  • Green – Things you’d associate with an octopus or squid
  • Blue – Things that have blades
  • Purple – Things you might nickname ‘cab‘

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, April 24:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, April 24 (#1048)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – CHEESE, DOUGH, PEPPERONI, TOMATO SAUCE
  • Green – ARMS, INK, INTELLIGENCE, SUCTION CUPS
  • Blue – GRASS, HELICOPTER, ICE SKATES, LAWN MOWER
  • Purple – CABIN, CALLOWAY, RED WINE, TAXI

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-april-24-1048/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Behaviour Interactive Lays Off Employees, Microsoft Plans Buyout Offers for 7% of Workforce
item.content:encoded:
Behaviour Interactive, the developer behind Dead By Daylight and more, has confirmed a round of layoffs. This also comes as Microsoft has a plan to offer around 7% of employees voluntary buyouts.

As for the Behaviour Interactive cuts, the company confirmed that they had laid off staff but did not mention or confirm any numbers. In a statement, a company spokesperson said, 

“A portion of Behaviour Interactive’s business has traditionally focused on external development partnerships. In recent months, demand for mobile and casual external development projects has declined. As we conclude our remaining engagements and do not foresee comparable opportunities in the near term, we have made the decision to part ways with some colleagues.”

The company says they will support affected employees in some way. Dead By Daylight continues going strong, with the company revealing a new, expanded Age of Titan collaboration.

Microsoft isn’t laying off again just yet. They’re planning to offer buyout packages to about 7% of their workforce in May, according to CNBC. These voluntary buyouts are the first time Microsoft has done this. According to the CNBC report, eligibility criteria is being at the senior director level or below, with a total combination of age and years at the company with a sum of 70 or above. May 7th looks to be the date when eligible employees will get their offers.

“Our hope is that this program gives those eligible the choice to take that next step on their own terms, with generous company support,” a memo from a Microsoft SVP says.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/behaviour-interactive-lays-off-employees-microsoft-plans-buyout-offers-for-7-of-workforce-2000137890
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox Game Pass ‘Starter Edition’ Tied to Discord Nitro Leaks Online
item.content:encoded:
Recently, Microsoft teased an incoming partnership of some kind between Xbox and Discord. In an all-new leak, that collaboration appears to have been revealed. It’s an all-new Xbox Game Pass tier called ‘Starter Edition’ that’s set to be bundled with Discord Nitro.

Per the claims, Xbox Game Pass Starter Edition will come with access to ‘over 50 games’ from the existing library, likely curated from an array of first- and third-party titles. An image that leaked alongside the first reports online showed titles like Fallout 4, Stardew Valley, Gears, and Hades, amongst others.

Xbox and Discord Collab Surfaces Online

Earlier today, a dataminer with the handle ‘redphx’ uncovered an official name for the rumored ‘Triton’ or ‘Duet’ Game Pass tiers. Last week, these codenames surfaced, with many assuming a partnership between a major platform provider was in the works, and the running theory was a collaboration between Xbox and Netflix.

But that user above revealed that Triton/Duet is named Starter Edition.

In a follow-up report from The Verge, more information was shared, this time provided by Discord Previews, another datamining profile that unearths and publishes upcoming Discord tidbits.

It was claimed that the Xbox Game Pass Starter Edition will include up to ten hours a month of Xbox Cloud Gaming streaming and the ability to earn Xbox Rewards while playing on the service. The Xbox Rewards scheme is set to receive an upgrade soon, allowing users to spend earned points on the Xbox Store.

This tier, which is the fruit of a fresh crossover effort, will see Discord Nitro users acquire a minimal Xbox Game Pass subscription via the Nitro Rewards programme. If you’re a Discord Nitro subscriber, you’ll soon be able to bolt on the Game Pass tier for free.

Do you think this new Xbox Game Pass tier is a win for Discord Nitro subscribers? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Conan Exiles is getting a lewd new feature in the Enhanced edition

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/xbox-game-pass-starter-edition-leaks/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pixel art combo-packed character action game Knuckle Paradise announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

From ScourgeBringer developer Flying Oak Games.

Publisher ARTE France and ScourgeBringer developer Flying Oak Games have announced Knuckle Paradise a pixel art “non-stop character action game” inspired by classic Hong Kong action comedies. It will be available for PC via Steam. A release date was not announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Oh no! Jane has been abducted and forced into an underground fighting tournament! Punch your way with stylish combos and your sidekick… battle-chicken?! Freely explore an abandoned amusement park filled with drama and absurd situations in this non-stop character action game inspired by classic Hong Kong action comedies.

Spectacular Action, Non-Stop Drama, and… A Chicken?!

From the developers of ScourgeBringer and NeuroVoider comes Knuckle Paradise—a precise and juicy hand-drawn spectacle fighter! Inspired by classic Hong Kong action comedies and character action games (you know Dante and Bayo, right?): Knuckle Paradise combat is fast, it’s furious, it’s combo-based, and it’s so aerial you’ll send Jane—and Jane’s enemies—flying!

Play as Jane, a retired boxer who finds herself stranded on an abandoned amusement park island! Has she been abducted?! It’s worse than that: she’s been forced into an underground martial arts tournament taking place within the park!

It won’t take long for Jane to team up with Cocotte, a warrior princess chicken. Together, the duo will battle their way through the mysteries of the island, encountering and defeating powerful opponents along the way.

Use Cocotte’s special abilities to overpower your opponents in unexpected ways. Combat is satisfyingly punchy and immediately fun… while remaining accessible for many skill levels.

Freely Explore the Park and Have a Combo-Packed Adventure!

Are you a completionist who wants to explore, do side quests, and meet new characters? We’ve got loads of that! Find the hidden martial art magazines and unlock new skills for Cocotte while looking for protein bars to increase Jane’s maximum health. Infiltrate a night club with your stealth skills, participate in car chases, get lost in the mirror house…

Do you want to stick to brawling and not get distracted? Go for it! The park is full of hand-animated combatants who are all cruising for a bruising, plus you’re sure to run into some real trophy-seeking challengers in their big, open, boss-room-like areas. You’ll also meet a crew of endearing and quirky characters while beating your way through this story-rich, chicken-forward, deliciously dynamic character action adventure.

Watch the announcement teaser trailer below. Visit the official website here.

Announce Teaser Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/pixel-art-combo-packed-character-action-game-knuckle-paradise-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Black Desert Online Arena of Solare Season Launching Next Week, NA vs. EU Showdown Regionals This Weekent
item.content:encoded:
Black Desert Online is launching the next Arena of Solare season next week, with improvements to make it more enjoyable. PvP balance changes are on the way, there’s a new hanges to quests, events, and more. Plus, we have some info on the Heidel Ball’s NA vs EU Showdown.

The new season of Arena of Solare  begins on April 30th. Pearl Abyss is planning to share more details tomorrow in the Global Lab update notice with the full list of changes, but we can expect a new ally location HUD, Some adjustments to score rules, better rewards, new battlefields, and more changes.

There will also be some class balance tweaks coming to Global Lab. The team is changing their policy of not making PVP balance changes during a regular Arena of Solare season, instead they're going to keep an eye out and make adjustments early in the season if there's some pressing changes that need to be made to relieve over or underperforming elements. 

Other changes include the new Extra AP Against Monsters (previously “against all species”)And the patch notes have more info on the  changes, but this time you'll be able to check your exact total AP against various monsters when you view total stats by using the new no species damage filter.

Some cleanup is happening for Balenos main quests after a revamp. Those with characters still on the pre-revamp questline will be able to continue on the new version.

Black Desert Online players that have logged in with Facebook won’t be able to do that anymore starting in June.

Finally, we now have info on the NA vs. EU Showdown 3v3 PvP tournament  happening at Heidel Ball, which is happening in California in honor of the 10th anniversary of Western release.  the preliminary Regional Tournament kicks off this weekend and you can watch live on stream. you can check out all of the teams participating, along with their rosters and their chosen classes here in the patch notes. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/black-desert-online-arena-of-solare-season-launching-next-week-na-vs-eu-showdown-regionals-this-weekent-2000137889
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Former PlayStation head isn't sure how Sony can recoup those big budgets without first-party games coming to PC
item.content:encoded:
And he's not seen proof of a strategy change, either

Last month, a report emerged that claimed that Sony would no longer be putting its first-party games like Ghost of Yotei on PC, with online games like Marathon being the exception. The PlayStation maker is yet to confirm or deny this, and the report even noted that Sony's strategy is "constantly shifting," meaning this may end up proving untrue. But former Sony Interactive Entertainment Worldwide Studios boss Shuhei Yoshida says he's not seen any proof of PlayStation changing their strategy this console generation.

Yoshida recently made an appearance at Powerhouse Museum's ALT. Games Festival as a keynote speaker, and took part in an interview with the Back Pocket podcast (ta, Respawn First) where he spoke of PlayStation's shift to PC. "When I was working on the game development side, first-party at PlayStation, from a strategy standpoint, we [were] not allowed to bring our triple A games to other platforms like PC," Yoshida explained. He goes on to note that with the increase of game development scale and costs that it made sense to him that during the PS5 generation first-party games started releasing on PC.

The big thing with this move, to Yoshida, is the ability to help bring money back in after those big expenditures. "Releasing games on PC after a couple of years must have helped recoup the investment of these big budget games and help the team and company to reinvest that money into their new games," he said. It's on this point that he says that he's "not seeing any proof of them changing the strategy this generation," but if they are, "it's going to be interesting to [see] how they are able to maintain the investment on the big budget games on [the] first-party side going forward."

Leaks related to Marvel's Spider-Man 2 positioned it as having a $300 million budget, making it one of the most expensive games of all time. While PlayStation have struggled with their PC releases, often due to a mixture of flat out bad ports, keeping that completely off the table does seem like a potentially odd move. If there's no sign of Ghost of Yotei on PC by the end of 2027, and no official word from Sony, perhaps then we'll have our answer.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/former-playstation-head-isnt-sure-how-sony-can-recoup-those-big-budgets-without-first-party-games-coming-to-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Perfect Tides: Station to Station for Switch launches May 14
item.content:encoded:
• Switch

Narrative adventure game currently available for PC.

Narrative adventure game Perfect Tides: Station to Station will launch for Switch on May 14, developer Three Bees announced.

Perfect Tides: Station to Station first launched for PC via Steam on January 22.

“I wanted to make sure that players have a smooth and seamless experience playing Perfect Tides: Station to Station on Switch, so we worked with Clickpulp to get the Switch controls just right,” said Three Bees founder Meredith Gran in a press release. “They’ve done a great job, and Mara feels right at home on the Nintendo Switch.”

Here is an overview of the game, via Three Bees:

Meredith Gran, creator of the hit webcomic Octopus Pie and the award winning Perfect Tides, fills each chapter of Perfect Tides: Station to Station with unforgettable characters and authentic life. Join fledgling writer Mara Whitefish as she begins her college years in the big city, where her competing desires to become a successful writer and be loved by others propels her into a series of tumultuous experiences and encounters that shape who she becomes as a person.

Navigate Mara’s newfound independence to balance education, relationships, and personal development as she struggles to find her footing as a young adult. Talk with new friends, lovers, and mentors to deepen relationships, and use the city’s mass transit system to explore more than 60 environments.

Keep track of names in Mara’s early 2000s’ brick-style cell phone, and collect important books and reading assignments to broaden her knowledge of new topics to discuss up with everyone she encounters. Each experience, assignment, and conversation present new opportunities for Mara to grow into the writer she dreams of becoming—or languish in mediocre obscurity.

Watch a new trailer below.

Switch Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/perfect-tides-station-to-station-for-switch-launches-may-14
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Echo Generation 2 Reveals Characters, Gameplay, and More Of An Intertwined Story - Interview
item.content:encoded:
Today at the Second Wind Showcase, a new trailer for Echo Generation 2 was shown off, revealing a variety of new characters and locations in this sequel/prequel to the charming and strange Echo Generation by Cococucumber.

Ahead of the reveal, I learned more about the newly revealed characters and had a chance to speak with Vanessa Chia, co-founder and producer at Cococucumber. I got to learn more about the scope of Echo Generation 2, how each of these new characters might alter your playstyle, and how this time around the game will take a slightly more mature tone.

The trailer, opening on what was meant to be a nice vacation for Jack and his family, takes a creepy turn as strange lights and explosions appear behind them while posing for a photo. From there, we're walked through combat moments in a variety of different settings where our heroes face off against some unsettling foes.

Echo Generation 2 Features Characters From Different Worlds

The character pairings that we get to see are Jack, "a former special ops agent turned family man;" Bulder "a mischievous creature from another world;" Annata Z, "a wandering exile who must piece together her lost memories on a stark industrial frontier;" Noliva, "a charismatic bounty hunter;" Sister M, "a defiant psychic teenager determined to escape the FST Research Center;" and Strix, a cat-like creature "rescued from a deadly storm and has been inseparable from Noliva ever since."

If you're interested in getting hands-on with Echo Generation 2 there is a demo currently available on Steam and Xbox. 

Elaborating on the story structure and where each of these characters comes in, Chia let me know, "The game is structured into character chapters, and each one highlights a different part of the world with its own tone and atmosphere. In Noliva’s chapter, you explore a neon-lit cyberpunk city, while Annata Z’s story takes place in a harsh, noir-inspired industrial wasteland out in the outer colonies. There is a wide range of settings and moods across the game. As the story progresses, these characters and their worlds start to connect, eventually converging at a key moment."

This explains a lot of the state of the trailer as you see Jack fighting floating mouthy enemies in a parking lot while Annata Z is in a monochrome hellscape, hiding from a gigantic winged humanoid robot out at sea.

Chia was also able to explain to me how gameplay would be different between each of these characters.

"Jack focuses on setting up status effects for allies over longer fights, while Bulder is a high-risk, high-reward burst DPS who thrives at low health, and Noliva delivers powerful single-target damage through careful card sequencing and stance switching.

"Annata Z fills a durable, status-spreading tank role, Strix supports the team by converting enemy debuffs into buffs, and Sister M has high multi-target damage and a wide spread of status effects.

"It all comes down to building a team that complements each other."

At any time, players will only be able to have three characters in their party, but by allowing characters to be focused on for certain chapters before being brought together in the story, the players will get more than enough time to understand the mechanics of each before getting to see how they fight before mixing and matching.

Chia did warn me, "If a character falls in combat, they are not replaced by a reserve, so team composition and planning really matter."

In moving Echo Generation from a traditional turn-based system into a deckbuilder, the team at Cococucumber saw the potential for added strategy for players to dive into. "Moving to a full deckbuilding system felt like a natural evolution that opens up more player choice and experimentation. Status effects were already part of the first game, so we saw an opportunity to expand on that. Over time, they became a core part of the combat design, allowing for deeper combos and more meaningful interactions between characters."

Echo Generation 2 Is a Larger and More Mature Adventure

"From the start, we were interested in telling a story on a much bigger, more galactic scale. That shift also came with a change in perspective. In Echo Generation 2, we follow Jack, the father, instead of the kids. Naturally, that led us toward a darker tone and a more mature storyline that fits his character."

Knowing that we're following Jack, the father, I was interested to know how much overlap there might be between the world of Echo Generation that we know, and the world of the past that we'll be exploring in Echo Generation 2.

"Players will revisit familiar locations but at different points in time. The FST research facility is a great example. It plays a major role in the sequel and shows up across multiple character stories. There are also plenty of references and easter eggs tied to the first game. We had a lot of fun weaving those in, and we are excited to see players spot them."

Speaking to the inspirations behind Echo Generation 2, Chia broke it down into two categories: tone and gameplay. 

"A big part of Echo Generation’s identity comes from our love of '80s and '90s sci-fi films and anime. Things like Blade Runner, Back to the Future, The Thing, and Akira have all influenced the tone and worldbuilding. We try to take that inspiration and reinterpret it in a way that feels unique to Echo Generation.

"On the gameplay side, combat draws from a mix of genres. JRPGs like Octopath Traveler, deckbuilders like Slay the Spire, and adventure games like Another World all played a role in shaping the experience."

Echo Generation 2 will launch on May 27 on Steam, Xbox Series X|S, Xbox on PC, Xbox Cloud, and will be on Xbox Game Pass and is an Xbox Play Anywhere title. It's expected that the game should take players 8-10 hours to complete, but with a larger world and more combat customization you could spend a lot longer refining your strategies.

Andrew has written Video Game and Entertainment news, reviews, and guides for 10+ years. As Senior Content Manager, he assists in creating and editing… More about Andrew



https://techraptor.net/gaming/features/echo-generation-2-reveals-characters-gameplay-and-more-of-intertwined-story
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Looks Even Better In Side-By-Side Comparisons With The Original
item.content:encoded:
After a lot of past leaks, rumors, and reports, Ubisoft has officially revealed Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, a complete remake of the original Black Flag. Built using Ubisoft’s latest version of its in-house Anvil engine, this upcoming remake looks great. But when you compare it directly against the original 13-year-old game, Resynced looks even better.

On April 23, Ubisoft released a ton of screenshots and gameplay of Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, which is set to arrive on Xbox, PlayStation, and PC on July 9. During the initial reveal, Ubisoft showcased some direct splitscreen comparisons of the original and the remake, and the new game looks incredible, which isn’t surprising as it’s not over a decade old and designed to run on Xbox 360 and Xbox One. Still, the amount of extra detail and the improved lighting in the new screenshots is impressive.

Faces are improved in Black Flag Desynced 

A lot of the original visuals and models for Black Flag still hold up better than you might expect. But, as shown perfectly in a comparison video from YouTube channel Enfant Terrible, one thing that looks decidedly rough today is character faces. That’s been greatly improved in the remake.

It’s not just Edward Kennway that gets a nice glow-up in the remake. Adéwalé, a former slave and badass pirate-assassin himself, also looks more detailed in the remake.

Cities look bigger and better in the remake

Cities in Black Flag weren’t a huge part of the game compared to past titles at the time, as the pirate-themed sequel focused on islands and ocean environments. Still, there are some gorgeous cities in the first Black Flag, and they are all back and fully recreated and looking quite nice.

It’s not just sweeping vistas and faraway skylines that look better, but the nitty-gritty details, too. The original Black Flag‘s environments and cities still look pretty dang good all these years later, but compared to the more detailed, higher-res textures and models seen in the remake, it’s clear how much video game visuals have advanced since 2013.

Streets will also feature more detailed paths, extra NPCs, and improved draw distances, as well.

Forts and islands look much better, too

As you might expect, some of the best-looking parts of the original Black Flag and the remake are out on the open waters. Islands and forts make up a big part of both games, and while the original Black Flag had some impressive models and scenery, the remake looks even more remarkable. This is best seen in screenshots comparing this particular fort found in both versions.

And here are a variety of screenshots showing off how the Jackdaw, Edward’s main pirate ship, looks in both the OG game and the remake. Click or tap the first image to open the full gallery and easily flip between the screenshots.

And perhaps the most striking comparison image of the bunch shows a villa on an island. Of all the images, this one shows the leap in visual fidelity from the original Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag and the upcoming remake.

I know there will be some who claim the new remake looks worse or lacks soul. And I get it. We’ve seen plenty of video game remakes and remasters that have ruined the original game’s style and look. It’s something I’ve complained about on this very site!

But after flipping through all the new Resynced gameplay and comparing it to the original Black Flag, I think Ubisoft has knocked the visuals in the remake out of the park. The lighting changes look incredible. Black Flag could sometimes look a bit flat, and combined with lower draw distance, it sometimes felt like the game was struggling to pull off its vast and sunny pirate sandbox. Here, in the remake, Ubisoft seems able to fully commit to not only luscious trees and cramped cities, but bathe all of that in lots of warm and bright sunlight, as well as crank up the detail. This is what Black Flag looked like in my brain before I saw the remake, and that’s all I want out of a remake, assuming a studio is making one. I’d rather have a new game using those resources, but that’s a whole different story.

Of course, if you find the new visuals off-putting or not to your liking, don’t worry. Ubisoft has confirmed that it has no plans to remove the original game from PC or console. So you can go back and enjoy that version if you prefer. For those who want the new stuff, you don’t have to wait long. Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced arrives on consoles and PC on July 9.



https://kotaku.com/assassins-creed-black-flag-resynced-side-by-side-visual-graphics-comparisons-ubisoft-2000690216
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Echo Generation 2 details characters
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Xbox Series

Meet Jack, Bulder, Annata Z, and more.

Cococucumber has released a new trailer for cyberpunk turn-based RPG Echo Generation 2 introducing the game’s cast.

Here is an overview of the game and each character, via Cococucumber:

About

Echo Generation 2 follows Jack’s journey, introducing new characters such as Annata Z, Noliva, Strix, Sister M, and Bulder through interconnected chapters and changing perspectives. The turn-based deckbuilder features more than 150 cards, enabling players to wreak havoc with devastating combos, from protective shields to perfectly timed summons.

Characters

  • Jack – Echo Generation 2‘s main protagonist and the driving force behind the narrative. A former special ops agent turned family man, he is pulled back into conflict after a catastrophic breach tears at reality. As the world fractures, Jack must protect his family from escalating mutations and encroaching cosmic horrors from other dimensions.
  • Bulder – A mischievous creature from another world, cast onto Earth through the breach. Driven by a shared desire to return home, Bulder joins Jack on a perilous mission deep within the FST. As they uncover hidden experiments, they are drawn into a web of secrets where Dr. Lee, an old acquaintance, plays a dangerous game of trust.
  • Annata Z – A wandering exile who must piece together her lost memories on a stark industrial frontier. As fragments of her memories surface, they reveal a trail of loss and suffering. Fueled by grief and resolve, her journey becomes a relentless pursuit of vengeance for her daughter and for the suffering of her people.
  • Noliva – A charismatic bounty hunter who thrives in the chaos of the Neon District alongside her loyal companion, Strix. When echoes of her hidden past emerge, she is forced to confront reality, and the crime boss who raised her. The truth he holds could reshape everything she thought she knew, and the future of the galaxies.
  • Sister M – A defiant psychic teenager determined to escape the FST Research Center at any cost. As she searches for her missing friends, she uncovers disturbing truths buried within the facility. What she discovers reveals not only the institute’s purpose, but her role in the center of it all. Sister M was first introduced during the game’s demo.
  • Strix – Rescued from a deadly storm and has been inseparable from Noliva ever since. Raised together in the mean streets of Sectoris under the influence of a powerful crime lord, the duo forged an unbreakable bond. Now they take on dangerous bounties across a neon-lit city divided by rival factions battling for control.

Echo Generation 2 is due out for Xbox Series and PC via Steam and Microsoft Store on May 27. It will also be available via Game Pass.

Watch the trailer below.

Characters Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/echo-generation-2-details-characters
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Stormforge launches in Early Access in 2026
item.content:encoded:
• PC

New gameplay trailer released.

Cel-shaded fantasy survival crafting game Stormforge will launch in Early Access for PC via Steam in 2026, developer Roboto Games announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Roboto Games:

Stormforge is a cooperative survival experience that supports up to 8 players, and is set in a world overrun by magical storms. Each storm presents unique dangers such as: tornadoes that fling rocks, trees, and enemies into the air; dust storms that reduce visibility and damage the player.

The storms have unique mechanics that change how the game is played, and are brimming with powerful resources, but filled with deadly creatures. Players will fight, craft and survive as a warrior, mage, ranger or any combination of these with the ability to unlock character classes and unique skills and abilities.

Stormforge will have deep support for modding combined with procedural world generation, allowing survival crafting players to experience the genre in new and exciting ways. The title will allow explorers to easily play with a wide variety of equipment, enemies, points of interest, and game rules created directly by the community, all accessible without any separate downloads.

Watch a new trailer below.

Gameplay Trailer 2026



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/stormforge-launches-in-early-access-in-2026
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Prelude: Dark Pain launches in Early Access this summer
item.content:encoded:
Dark fantasy turn-based strategy RPG Prelude: Dark Pain will launch in Early Access for PC via Steam this summer, developer Quickfire Games announced.

A new trailer introduces the game’s lore and heroes, as well as showcases some of their strategic synergies.

Newly introduced factions include:

  • The Blue Nets – Led by Duocco, the protagonist’s Soren father-in-law, who are tasked with guarding the seas and southern coastline from bandits. This faction has warriors such as Tammais, a frontline bruiser who is outstanding in either attack or defense; Maya, a skilled archer that can summon magic animals; Urza, a high shaman capable of using powerful support magic, and Rufio, a mighty axe wielder who can slice enemies as well as grapple them towards him.
  • The Keepers of the Veil – The oldest of the clans, often referred to as Lords of Death; their duty is to protect and fix the veil between realms. Representing this clan, we find the mysterious Sade, a woman of few words, using a cross that allows communication with her divine companion, The Lady of the Cross.
  • Van Rigmars – This clan controls the west of Statera. They are the only clan capable of extracting and mastering blood crystals, relics that hold great magical power. From the Van Rigmars, Gala makes her presence known with a musket in one hand and a whip in the other, while also using blood crystal magic for attack power boosting.

Watch the trailer below.

Characters Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/prelude-dark-pain-launches-in-early-access-this-summer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fired Unknown Worlds execs withdraw claim that Krafton violated court order by announcing Subnautica 2 release date
item.content:encoded:
image via Unknown Worlds/Steam

Fired Unknown Worlds executives Max McGuire and Charlie Cleveland, as well as reinstated CEO Ted Gill, asked the Delaware State Court of Chancery to toss out a motion where they claimed Krafton violated the court's order by announcing the Subnautica 2 early access release date.

A lawyer for the three executives wrote in a letter to Delaware State Court of Chancery vice chancellor Lori Will, filed Wednesday and obtained by Game Developer, they "no longer contend that Krafton violated the Court's March 16, 2026 opinion"—a reversal of its accusations that Krafton leaked the Subnautica 2 early access release date to IGN without Gill's approval.

McGuire, Cleveland, and McGill had their side of things—that Krafton schemed to sabotage the Subnautica 2 early access timeline to avoid paying $250 million in payouts—affirmed by the court in March, when vice chancellor Lori Will found Krafton illegally fired Gill to take over the Subnautica 2 studio. Gill was reinstated, but his two colleagues were not. Shortly after the court decision was made, Krafton and Unknown Worlds said in a letter published by IGN that Subnautica 2 would be released into early access in May. The letter was signed by Steve Papoutsis, who took over Unknown Worlds after Gill was originally fired. The three executives immediately filed documents with the court in which they argued Krafton should be sanctioned for violating the court order; Gill should be in charge of making such announcements.

Related:Canada federal government concerned about children's safety in Roblox

"Krafton self-servingly announced the launch without any regard to its impact on the game, the team, or the community—let alone this Court’s Opinion," lawyers wrote in the March filing.

Lawyers for the executives have continued to file in support of sanctions since then, including a letter from Gill himself, filed later in March but made public in the court docket on April 1, that he's slowly been regaining access to Unknown Worlds' systems.

"I was extremely dismayed to learn from these communications that Krafton had rushed to leak the timeline of the launch after the Opinion came out," Gill wrote. "A successful Early Access launch requires careful planning and preparation to foster authentic fan participation and support—as the Unknown Worlds' team's work reflects. Krafton's decision to make the announcement itself, in the manner that it did, was not in line with best practices and may well undermine the Subnautica 2 launch."

Krafton argued, in its own filing on March 22, that it disagreed. Krafton didn't violate anything. Papoutsis, Krafton's lawyers said, was just expressing excitement for the milestones Subnautica 2 had reached, and was welcoming Gill back to the company. The filing did not address allegations, made by Unknown Worlds lawyers in March, that the company leaked the document to IGN.

Related:The Super Mario Galaxy Movie earns $747M at global box office

In the Wednesday filing, the Unknown Worlds executives' lawyer asked the court to cancel an evidentiary hearing regarding the sanction motion from the calendar, and to "consider all pending requests for sanctions in the case as moot." Game Developer has reached out to the three plaintiffs and Krafton for comment.

Though Gill was reinstated and this matter was resolved, the lawsuit is far from over. The court broke its decision-making into two parts; the first was largely found in the executives favor, affirming their version of events. The next part of the court case will determine if Krafton's actions mean the executives will be awarded damages for the company's actions.

About the Author

Columnist, Game Developer

Nicole Carpenter is a veteran reporter who has been covering the video game industry for over a decade. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/fired-unknown-worlds-execs-withdraw-claim-that-krafton-violated-court-order-by-announcing-subnautica-2-release-date
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Quinfall is Adding a 3v3 PvP Arena and Ending PvP Zone Fees
item.content:encoded:
The Quinfall is going to add 3v3 Arena PvP and make the PvP zone free to enter in the next update. The team announced how the arena will work, some info on rewards, structure, and matchmaking, as well as tweaks to the original arena.

While there was no date for when we’ll see this update, Vawraek says that player feedback had a role in their final decision to add the new PvP option. The 3v3 arena will feature a best of three match format, with queue-based matchmaking. There will be no entry fee to participate.

Each team can have only one tank and one healer for balance reasons. Gear will be normalized to make sure it's a skills test, and there will be no consumables within the arena, including scrolls. If you win or lose, you'll still receive some kind of reward.  Vawraek is also planning to introduce a weekly league system to give highly-ranked competitors some high-value rewards. They’ll be taking action to help prevent unfair practices like win trading, and promise penalties for cheaters. 

The team is also planning on a couple of other improvements coming in the next update. The PVP Zone will be free of charge to enter, and the regular arena rewards will be increased.

Check out the rest in the 3v3 arena announcement for more details. They continue to invite feedback to help keep the experience as fair and enjoyable as possible.

The Quinfall is on 90% discount (99 cents) on Steam through May 5th.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/the-quinfall-is-adding-a-3v3-pvp-arena-and-ending-pvp-zone-fees-2000137888
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Shapez 2 leaves the factory floor today as it launches into 1.0
item.content:encoded:
I delight in looking at shapes, I suspect we all do. They are the building blocks of life, often turned into literal building blocks handed to us as wee babes to help us make sense of the world. Games like Shapez 2, then, feel like a natural through line from this point, a mechanical orchestra that lets you put all sorts of shapes together to build factories that produce more shapes endlessly. And now that line has seen its end, as Shapez 2 has left early access today, arriving at its 1.0 release a little less than two years on from its original launch.

Shapez 2 (Electric Boogaloo?) is a factory-building game distilled to its most base elements: the factory building. There's no need to worry about resources or costs, you just build and build and build until you've automated your factory to such a degree that you can revel in all the shapes it produces without a worry in the world. Ollie was quite complimentative about it in his early access review, saying that it had "turned the holy trinity of factory games (Factorio, Satisfactory, Dyson Sphere Program) into a holy quartet."

With 1.0 comes some new stuff too. There's a whole new mode to try out called Manufacture Mode. "This mode addresses one of the biggest feedback points we got during early access: factories you needed to build for a specific shape were no longer being at later stages in the game," explains a Steam blog post. "In the new Manufacture Mode you’ll focus on re-building your Vortex Platform by trading shapes with Trade Stations and building large-scale, permanent factories." There are now achievements, too, and modding support to boot.

You'll find a huge range of other changes, like visual improvements, tweaks to Classic Mode, a new tutorial, and much more besides. You can read about all the other additions right here in that aforementioned Steam blog post, and if the game takes your conceptual fancy, you can pick it up on Steam now.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/shapez-2-leaves-the-factory-floor-today-as-it-launches-into-10
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox Plans to Rework Game Pass with Sustainable Economics
item.content:encoded:
Services are a key pillar of the Xbox brand. Now, the company is focused on re-strengthening one of its main sources of growth and interaction, the Xbox Game Pass service.

Xbox has laid out its core priorities for the future in the new era led by Asha Sharma. One of the major focuses remains the reworking of the Game Pass subscription service as Microsoft reconfigures one of its key pillars of financial growth amid this period of economic uncertainty.

Recently, Sharma announced the decision to lower the price of the Game Pass Ultimate tier. Now, a new company-wide memo further details the main objectives of the refocused Xbox brand, including continued efforts to strengthen Game Pass,

Xbox Now Aims to Fortify Game Pass in Affordability

The memo, credited to both Asha Sharma and Xbox CCO Matt Booty, outlines the brand’s four priorities moving forward. Those priorities are hardware, content, experience, and services. As it pertains to Services, the first key objective is to “fortify Game Pass with clear differentiation and sustainable economics.”

It was recently reported that Xbox was considering a more à la carte approach with Game Pass, letting players choose the elements of their individual subscription that matter to them. While this was not explicitly confirmed in the memo, it is noted that they want the brand to be built around affordability and open to personal choice.

In line with past comments from Sharma, the memo states that the north star moving forward will be the daily active Xbox player base.

Other key objectives for Xbox include expanding and strengthening the portfolio of franchises players love and improving the fundamentals for players and business partners. Additionally, all roads are still pointing to the next console, codenamed Project Helix, and they want to make sure their next hardware output leads the way in performance and the ability to play both console and PC games.

To learn more about the future of Xbox, click here to read about the brand reevaluating its approach to exclusivity. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor all things Xbox and report any major updates as they come.

How do you feel about all of these shake-ups in 2026 for the Microsoft wing of gaming? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/xbox-plans-to-rework-game-pass-with-sustainable-economics/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ID@Xbox Spring Showcase 2026 – Everything Announced
item.content:encoded:
The ID@Xbox Spring Showcase 2026 featured its fair share of world premieres of upcoming games and new looks at previously announced indie titles, across multiple kinds of games at different scales.

Similar to our round-ups of the Galaxies Spring Showcase and the Triple-I Initiative Showcase, this article will round-up all of the news and announcements from today's show, so you don't have to miss any of it.

You can also check out the show below to take it all in, or look at the list of announcements to see which catches your eye.

Everything Announced at ID@Xbox Spring Showcase 2026

  • There Are No Ghosts at the Grand - new gameplay reveal
  • Totopia
  • Vaporworld: Over the Mind - gameplay reveal, demo coming in June 2026
  • Mistfall Hunter - new gameplay, July 2026 release window reveal
  • Starseeker: Astroneer Expeditions - new gameplay reveal
  • Aphelion - new gameplay reveal
  • Lofsong - official reveal
  • Albion Online - launch and new gameplay showcase
  • Vampire Crawlers - launch showcase
  • Echo Generation 2 - gameplay and release date reveal
  • Tears of Metal - official announcement
  • Crashout Crew - new gameplay and release date reveal
  • RV There Yet? - Xbox Game Pass arrival
  • Escape Academy 2: Back 2 School - official announcement and gameplay reveal
  • Solo Leveling Arise Overdrive - new gameplay and story update
  • Deep Dish Dungeon - gameplay overview
  • Beastro - new gameplay, release date reveal
  • inKonbini - new gameplay reveal
  • Kalanoro - official reveal
  • Speedrunners 2: King of Speed - new gameplay and release window reveal
  • Golf With Your Friends 2 - official announcement and release window reveal
  • Screen Bound - new gameplay reveal

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/id-at-xbox-spring-showcase-2026-everything-announced/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
